pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh line 103 where file is created and saved
Verified in export_result.sh line 8 and task.json description line 3
Task-specific arbitrary value; 'Omicron Industries' appears to be a fictional company created for this benchmark
Valid ISO date format verified; common format for SLA documents; value is arbitrary but plausible
Standard 'four nines' SLA uptime target commonly used in industry; verified as realistic and standard
Standard service credit percentage; falls within industry norm of 10-50% range
Document analysis via zipfile inspection of setup_task.sh generated ODT file. Placeholders extracted from content.xml using regex pattern. Only 6 unique placeholders found in actual document, not 7.

Regex search of generated ODT content.xml found only '[CLIENT]' (uppercase), not '[Client]' (capital C only). Setup_task.sh line 49 creates '[CLIENT]' not '[Client]'. Task description mentions both as variants to handle, but only '[CLIENT]' is generated.

privileged_info_summary: The task generates a synthetic SLA document using odfpy with specific placeholder text to be replaced with user field variables. The document path (/home/ga/Documents/SLA_Draft_Sentinels.odt) and all variable values (Omicron Industries, October 1 2025, 99.99%, 25%) are task-specific arbitrary choices. However, the metadata incorrectly claims a '[Client]' placeholder (with only capital C) exists in the document, when the actual generated document only contains '[CLIENT]' in all uppercase. The metadata lists 7 placeholders but only 6 are present in the generated document.
